intro:
I couldn't find any information on a plant called "Ericoila flava." However, I did find information on a plant called "Erica flava", also known as "Bell Heather". 

Here is a paragraph about Erica flava:

Erica flava, also known as Bell Heather, is a flowering plant species within the heather family (Ericaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Erica flava include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Erica flava is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures to alleviate respiratory issues, and its extracts are also used in the production of herbal cough drops and lozenges.
